title: ophrys aranifera subsp. aesculapii
intro: Ophrys aranifera subsp. aesculapii is a rare subspecies of the spider orchid plant, characterized by its unique, insect-like appearance. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. In herbal preparations, Ophrys aranifera subsp. aesculapii is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its availability and usage may vary due to concerns about its rarity and potential toxicity.
